signals.reasoning: BTC is trying to stabilize after the weekend selloff, but the active geopolitical veto and still-fragile macro tape mean this is not a clean fresh long or short. The honest read is watch the rebound, not force a trade.

ETH has bounced slightly off the flush, but the higher-timeframe structure is still damaged and the current move does not yet clear into a trustworthy continuation long. Under veto, patience is the better choice.
SOL is showing a small rebound from the flush lows, but it is still below the broken higher-timeframe range and not attractive enough for a new short either. This sits in no-trade territory for now.

IP has some squeeze fuel from negative funding, but the chart is still a weak bounce inside a damaged structure and the open-interest crowding is high. It is a watchlist candidate, not a real cleared trade.
BLAST has squeeze ingredients, but the liquidity is still poor and the tape is choppy rather than directional. That makes it too fragile to promote under the current veto regime.
BLUR is basing after the selloff, but neither the squeeze case nor the continuation-short case is strong enough here. The structure is still indecisive.
FTT is too thin and too structurally noisy to trust. Even if it screens as bearish on the sheet, it does not clear the bar for a real trade recommendation.

GOAT has very strong negative funding and a violent washout profile, which raises squeeze risk more than it creates a clean entry. This is exactly the kind of board leader that can fool us into fake conviction, so HOLD is the honest answer.
MERL remains soft, but the downside already looks like a completed liquidation move rather than a fresh continuation trigger. Not strong enough to act on under veto.
DASH is rebounding from a deep flush, which makes new shorts late and new longs early. It stays on the watchlist, but I would not put on a fresh position here.
HEMI is still one of the weaker charts, but after such a large flush it now has too much squeeze risk for a clean fresh short. The setup is not good enough to promote into a real trade right now.
